title: Worker processes are crashing
description: Alerts on any NGINX Plus worker process respawn within a 15-minute window, which indicates OOM kills, segfaults, or broken config reloads that drop in-flight requests.

definition.name: [NGINX] Worker processes are crashing
definition.message:
{{#is_alert}}
## 🚨 What's happening?

NGINX on **{{host.name}}** has abnormally terminated and respawned {{value}} worker process(es) in the last 15 minutes. Any non-zero respawn count indicates an unhealthy NGINX deployment.
{{/is_alert}}

{{#is_recovery}}
## ✅ Recovered

`nginx.processes.respawned_count` on {{host.name}} has resolved. No further worker respawns detected.
{{/is_recovery}}

{{^is_recovery}}

---

## 📈 Impact

Each worker crash drops all in-flight requests handled by that worker, causing client-visible connection resets and errors. Repeated respawning degrades overall NGINX throughput and may signal an impending full outage.

---

## Why?

Possible causes:
* **OOM kill** — A worker process exceeded its memory limit and was killed by the OS out-of-memory killer, typically due to large request buffers or memory leaks in a module.
* **Segfault or module bug** — A bug in a third-party NGINX module or the NGINX binary itself caused a segmentation fault, forcing the master process to respawn the worker.
* **Broken config reload** — A `nginx -s reload` with an invalid configuration caused workers to exit before the master process could cleanly replace them.
* **File descriptor exhaustion** — Workers hit the system `nofile` limit and crashed when unable to open new connections or log files.

---

## How to troubleshoot and resolve the issue?

1. **Identify the scope** — Confirm which `{{host.name}}` is affected and check if multiple hosts are impacted simultaneously, which would suggest a rollout or config change.
2. **Review recent metrics** — Inspect `nginx.processes.respawned_count` in [Metrics Explorer](/metric/explorer?exp_metric=nginx.processes.respawned_count&exp_scope=host:{{host.name}},env:{{env.name}}&exp_agg=sum&exp_type=line).
3. **Check system logs** — Review `/var/log/kern.log` or `dmesg` on `{{host.name}}` for OOM kill entries, and `/var/log/nginx/error.log` for segfault or reload error messages.
4. **Validate configuration** — Run `nginx -t` to confirm the current config is valid before any reload attempt.

### Cause and Resolution

| Cause | Resolution |
| ----- | ---------- |
| OOM kill | Increase worker memory limits or reduce per-worker buffer sizes in `nginx.conf` |
| Segfault or module bug | Identify the crashing module via core dump; update or disable the offending module |
| Broken config reload | Run `nginx -t` before reloading; roll back the last config change |
| File descriptor exhaustion | Increase `worker_rlimit_nofile` in `nginx.conf` and raise the system `nofile` ulimit |

---

## Related links

* [Documentation](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/nginx/)
* [Metrics Explorer](/metric/explorer?exp_metric=nginx.processes.respawned_count&exp_scope=host:{{host.name}},env:{{env.name}}&exp_agg=sum&exp_type=line)
* [Infrastructure](/infrastructure?filters=host:{{host.name}})
* [Logs](/logs?query=host:{{host.name}}+env:{{env.name}})
{{/is_recovery}}
